summary: Added final local release-readiness smoke coverage and documentation for the 48-hour MVP without public deployment. The new smoke test exercises the deterministic no-network MVP happy path through the real scan orchestrator boundary with an injected in-memory repository, verifies radar task context, mock SERP scan output with persisted opportunities, serializes an opportunity detail view model, and generates deterministic MVP Spec Markdown. README now documents fresh-checkout local validation, environment setup, Prisma validate/generate/migrate/seed commands, required tests/build/taskctl/diff checks, dev-server manual demo flow, and human-gated deployment notes. .env.example now separates required local MVP values from optional future provider, email, payment, cron, and deployment placeholders with no real secrets.

tests_run.result: PASS: 1 focused release-readiness smoke test passed. It blocked global fetch, ran the mock scan path, produced opportunities, serialized opportunity detail readiness, and generated deterministic MVP Spec Markdown.
PASS: 73 tests passed across 16 suites, including the new local release smoke.
PASS: package test script completed with 73 tests passing across 16 suites.
PASS: Next.js production build completed successfully. The local Windows build printed nonfatal @next/swc-win32-x64-msvc 'not a valid Win32 application' warnings before continuing and exiting 0.
PASS: taskctl validated 14 active task files, including M6-RELEASE-READINESS-SMOKE-001.
PASS: no whitespace errors. Git printed LF-to-CRLF working-copy warnings for .env.example and README.md.

decisions: Kept the smoke test fully local and deterministic by injecting an in-memory ScanRepository and using the default mock SERP path with explicit global fetch blocking.
Composed existing production boundaries instead of changing runtime behavior: runScan, serializeOpportunityDetail, and generateMvpSpec.
Documented the fresh-checkout validation sequence in README rather than adding package scripts, so package.json and package-lock.json remain unchanged.
Changed .env.example defaults to local mock-safe values and blank future-provider placeholders, removing fake real-looking API-key examples.
Documented manual demo steps against seeded task id seed-gamedev-microtools while keeping public deployment and account/provider setup out of scope.
risks: The local Windows build environment still emits nonfatal Next SWC optional-binary warnings even though build exits 0.
The README manual demo assumes the developer has run the documented local Prisma migrate and seed commands before starting the dev server.
Public deployment, production auth/session/ownership, paid providers, CI/CD, and commercial release are intentionally deferred and require human review before execution.
non_goal_confirmation: No public deployment, DNS, GitHub Actions, CI/CD, store listing, paid provider setup, or account creation was performed.
No package dependency, package-lock, Prisma schema, migration, seed, database reset, auth/session/ownership, billing, telemetry, public sharing, cron, or email-sending change was made.
No runtime changes were made to agents, services, lib, app routes, components, API behavior, scoring, scan orchestration, or provider adapters.
No real secrets were added; .env.example contains only local defaults and blank optional future placeholders.
requested_action: Review the release-readiness smoke, README, and .env.example for secrets/deployment posture, human-gated production boundaries, and no-network local MVP validation claims.
